spec_sections.title: CONCRETE FORMING AND ACCESSORIES
spec_sections.work_description:
PART 1 - GENERAL 
1.1 
SUMMARY 
A. 
Section Includes: 
1. 
Form-facing material for cast-in-place concrete. 
2. 
Shoring, bracing, and anchoring. 
1.2 
PREINSTALLATION MEETINGS 
A. 
Preinstallation Conference: Conduct conference at Project site. 
1.3 
ACTION SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Product Data: For each of the following: 
1. 
Exposed surface form-facing material. 
2. 
Waterstops. 
B. 
Sustainable Design Submittals:  
1. 
Recycled content: For each product. 
2. 
Environmental Product Declaration: For each product. 
3. 
Health Product Declaration: For each product. 
4. 
Sourcing of Raw Materials: Corporate sustainability report for each manufacturer. 
5. 
Laboratory Test Reports: For liquid floor treatments and curing and sealing 
compounds, indicating compliance with requirements for low-emitting materials. 
C. 
Shop Drawings: Prepared by, and signed and sealed by, a qualified professional engineer 
responsible for their preparation, detailing fabrication, assembly, and support of forms. 
1. 
For exposed vertical concrete walls, indicate dimensions and form tie locations. 
2. 
Indicate dimension and locations of construction and movement joints required to 
construct the structure in accordance with ACI 301. 
a. 
Location of construction joints is subject to approval of the Architect. 
3. 
Indicate location of waterstops.
1.4 
INFORMATIONAL SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Field quality-control reports. 
B. 
Minutes of preinstallation conference.
spec_sections.execution_requirements:
PART 3 - EXECUTION 
3.1 
INSTALLATION OF FORMWORK 
A. 
Comply with ACI 301. 
B. 
Construct formwork, so concrete members and structures are of size, shape, alignment, 
elevation, and position indicated, within tolerance limits of ACI 117 and to comply with 
the Surface Finish designations specified in Section 033000 "Cast-In-Place Concrete" for 
as-cast finishes. 
C. 
Limit concrete surface irregularities as follows: 
1. 
Surface Finish-1.0: ACI 117 Class D, 1 inch. 
2. 
Surface Finish-2.0: ACI 117 Class B, 1/4 inch. 
3. 
Surface Finish-3.0: ACI 117 Class A, 1/8 inch. 
D. 
Construct forms tight enough to prevent loss of concrete mortar. 
1. 
Minimize joints. 
2. 
Exposed Concrete: Symmetrically align joints in forms. 
E. 
Construct removable forms for easy removal without hammering or prying against 
concrete surfaces. 
1. 
Provide crush or wrecking plates where stripping may damage cast-concrete 
surfaces. 
2. 
Provide top forms for inclined surfaces steeper than 1.5 horizontal to 1 vertical. 
3. 
Install keyways, reglets, recesses, and other accessories, for easy removal. 
F. 
Do not use rust-stained, steel, form-facing material. 
G. 
Set edge forms, bulkheads, and intermediate screed strips for slabs to achieve required 
elevations and slopes in finished concrete surfaces. 
1. 
Provide and secure units to support screed strips. 
2. 
Use strike-off templates or compacting-type screeds.
H. 
Provide temporary openings for cleanouts and inspection ports where interior area of

compounds, indicating compliance with r

spec_sections.title: CONCRETE REINFORCING
spec_sections.work_description:
PART 1 - GENERAL 
1.1 
SUMMARY 
A. 
Section Includes: 
1. 
Steel reinforcement bars. 
2. 
Welded-wire reinforcement. 
3. 
Steel fiber reinforcement. 
4. 
Twisted Steel Micro Rebar. 
1.2 
PREINSTALLATION MEETINGS 
A. 
Preinstallation Conference: Conduct conference at Project site. 
1.3 
ACTION SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Product Data: For the following: 
1. 
Each type of steel reinforcement. 
2. 
Bar supports. 
3. 
Mechanical splice couplers. 
4. 
Steel fiber / Twisted Steel Micro Rebar (TSMR) reinforcement manufacturer’s 
standard product description and dosage and mixing instructions.  
B. 
Sustainable Design Submittals: 
1. 
Submit Environmental Product Declaration (EPD): 
a. 
Independent third-party verified. 
b. 
Global Warming Potential (GWP) measured in kgCO2e/kg, tracking 
construction materials stages A1-A3 embodied carbon emissions. Indicate 
inclusion or exclusion of stage A4 emissions. 
2. 
Maximum Allowable GWP: 
a. 
Embodied carbon emissions in stages A1-A3 for fabricated reinforcement 
products must not exceed 0.650 kilograms of CO2 equivalent per kilogram 
of reinforcement (kgCO2e/kg). 
3. 
Product Data: For recycled content, indicating postconsumer and preconsumer 
recycled content and cost. 
4. 
Health Product Declaration: For each product. 
5. 
Sourcing of Raw Materials: Corporate sustainability report for each manufacturer. 
C. 
Shop Drawings: Comply with ACI SP-066:
1. 
Include placing drawings that detail fabrication, bending, and placement. 
2. 
Include bar sizes, l
spec_sections.execution_requirements:
PART 3 - EXECUTION 
3.1 
PREPARATION 
A. 
Protection of In-Place Conditions: 
1. 
Do not cut or puncture vapor retarder. 
2. 
Repair damage and reseal vapor retarder before placing concrete. 
B. 
Clean reinforcement of loose rust and mill scale, earth, ice, and other foreign materials 
that reduce bond to concrete. 
3.2 
INSTALLATION OF STEEL REINFORCEMENT 
A. 
Comply with CRSI's "Manual of Standard Practice" for placing and supporting 
reinforcement. 
B. 
Accurately position, support, and secure reinforcement against displacement. 
1. 
Locate and support reinforcement with bar supports to maintain minimum concrete 
cover. 
2. 
Do not tack weld crossing reinforcing bars. 
C. 
Preserve clearance between bars of not less than 1 inch, not less than one bar diameter, 
or not less than 1-1/3 times size of large aggregate, whichever is greater. 
D. 
Provide concrete coverage in accordance with ACI 318. 
E. 
Set wire ties with ends directed into concrete, not toward exposed concrete surfaces. 
F. 
Splices: Lap splices as indicated on Drawings. 
1. 
Bars indicated to be continuous, and all vertical bars shall be lapped not less than 
36 bar diameters at splices, or 24 inches, whichever is greater. 
2. 
Stagger splices in accordance with ACI 318. 
G. 
Install welded-wire reinforcement in longest practicable lengths. 
1. 
Support welded-wire reinforcement in accordance with CRSI "Manual of Standard 
Practice." 
a. 
For reinforcement less than W4.0 or D4.0, continuous support spacing sh

spec_sections.title: PRECAST STRUCTURAL CONCRETE
spec_sections.execution_requirements:
PART 3 - EXECUTION 
3.1 
INSTALLATION 
A. 
Install clips, hangers, bearing pads, and other accessories required for connecting precast 
structural concrete units to supporting members and backup materials. 
B. 
Erect precast structural concrete level, plumb, and square within specified allowable 
tolerances. Provide temporary structural framing, shoring, and bracing as required to 
maintain position, stability, and alignment of units until permanent connections are 
complete. 
1. 
Maintain horizontal and vertical joint alignment and uniform joint width as 
erection progresses. 
2. 
Remove projecting lifting devices and use plastic patch caps or sand-cement grout 
to fill voids within recessed lifting devices flush with surface of adjacent precast 
surfaces when recess is exposed. 
C. 
Connect precast structural concrete units in position by bolting, welding, grouting, or as 
otherwise indicated on Shop Drawings. Remove temporary shims, wedges, and spacers 
as soon as practical after connecting and grouting are completed. 
D. 
Field cutting of precast units is not permitted without approval of Architect. 
E. 
Fasteners: Do not use drilled or powder-actuated fasteners for attaching accessory items 
to precast, prestressed concrete units. 
F. 
Welding: Comply with applicable requirements in AWS D1.1/D1.1M and AWS 
D1.4/D1.4M for welding, welding electrodes, appearance, quality of welds, and methods 
used in correcting welding work. 
G. 
At bolted connections, use lock washers, t

spec_sections.title: PRECAST ARCHITECTURAL CONCRETE
spec_sections.work_description:
PART 1 - GENERAL 
1.1 
SUMMARY 
A. 
Section Includes: 
1. 
Precast architectural concrete units. 
2. 
Mold materials. 
3. 
Reinforcing materials. 
4. 
Prestressing tendons. 
5. 
Concrete materials. 
6. 
Steel connection materials. 
7. 
Grout materials. 
1.2 
PREINSTALLATION MEETINGS 
A. 
Preinstallation Conference: Conduct conference at Project site. 
1.3 
ACTION SUBMITTALS 
A. 
Product Data: For each type of product. 
B. 
Design Mixtures: For each precast concrete mixture. 
C. 
Shop Drawings: 
1. 
Detail fabrication and installation of architectural precast concrete units. 
2. 
Indicate locations, plans, elevations, dimensions, shapes, and cross sections of 
each unit. 
3. 
Indicate joints, reveals, drips, chamfers, and extent and location of each surface 
finish. 
4. 
Indicate details at building corners. 
D. 
Samples: Design reference samples for initial verification of design intent, for each type 
of finish indicated on exposed surfaces of architectural precast concrete units, in sets of 
three, representative of finish, color, and texture variations expected; approximately 12 
by 12 by 2 inches (300 by 300 by 50 mm).
E. 
Sample Panels: After sample approval and before fabricating architectural precast 
concrete units, produce a minimum of two sample panels approximately 16 sq. ft. (1.5 
sq. m) in area for review by Architect. Incorporate full-scale details of architectural 
features, finishes, textures, and transitions in sample panels. 
1. 
Locate panels where indicate
spec_sections.execution_requirements:
PART 3 - EXECUTION 
3.1 
INSTALLATION OF PRECAST ARCHITECTURAL CONCRETE UNITS 
A. 
Install clips, hangers, bearing pads, and other accessories required for connecting 
architectural precast concrete units to supporting members and backup materials. 
B. 
Erect architectural precast concrete level, plumb, and square within specified allowable 
tolerances. Provide temporary supports and bracing as required to maintain position, 
stability, and alignment of units until permanent connections are completed. 
1. 
Maintain horizontal and vertical joint alignment and uniform joint width as 
erection progresses. 
2. 
Unless otherwise indicated, maintain uniform joint widths of 3/4 inch (19 mm). 
C. 
Connect architectural precast concrete units in position by bolting, welding, grouting, or 
as otherwise indicated on Shop Drawings. Remove temporary shims, wedges, and spacers 
as soon as practical after connecting and grouting are completed. 
D. 
Welding: 
Comply 
with 
applicable 
requirements 
in 
AWS D1.1/D1.1M 
and 
AWS D1.4/D1.4M for welding, welding electrodes, appearance, quality of welds, and 
methods used in correcting welding work. 
E. 
At bolted connections, use lock washers, tack welding, or other approved means to 
prevent loosening of nuts after final adjustment. 
F. 
Grouting or Dry Packing Connections and Joints: Grout connections where required or 
indicated. Retain flowable grout in place until hard enough to support itself. 
Alternatively, pack spaces with stiff dry pac

spec_sections.title: HYDRAULIC CEMENT UNDERLAYMENT
spec_sections.work_description:
PART 1 - GENERAL 
1.1 SUMMARY 
A. Section Includes: 
1. Polymer-modified, self-leveling, hydraulic cement underlayment for application below 
interior floor coverings. 

1.2 PREINSTALLATION MEETINGS 
A. Preinstallation Conference: Conduct conference at Project site. 

1.3 ACTION SUBMITTALS 
A. Product Data: For each type of product. 
1. Sustainable Design Submittals: Product Data: For coatings, indicating VOC content. 
2. Laboratory Test Reports: For coatings, indicating compliance with requirements for 
low-emitting materials. 

1.4 QUALITY ASSURANCE 
A. Installer Qualifications: Installer who is approved by manufacturer for application of 
underlayment products required for this Project. 
1.5 FIELD CONDITIONS 
A. Environmental Limitations: Comply with manufacturer’s written instructions for substrate 
temperature, ventilation, ambient temperature and humidity, and other conditions affecting 
underlayment performance. 
1. Place hydraulic cement underlayments only when ambient temperature and temperature 
of substrates are between 50 and 80 deg F.
spec_sections.submittal_requirements:
A. Product Data: For each type of product. 
1. Sustainable Design Submittals: Product Data: For coatings, indicating VOC content. 
2. Laboratory Test Reports: For coatings, indicating compliance with requirements for 
low-emitting materials.

takeoff_items.description: Concrete formwork - exposed vertical concrete walls (form-facing material, Surface Finish-2.0 or 3.0)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit wall area given; estimated based on typical mid-size commercial building with exposed concrete walls. Quantity to be confirmed from architectural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Concrete formwork - slabs on grade and elevated slabs (edge forms, bulkheads, screed strips)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit slab area stated; estimated based on typical commercial building footprint. Confirm from structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Concrete formwork - footings and foundations
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit footing perimeter or depth given; estimated for typical commercial structure. Confirm from structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Waterstops - at construction joints in concrete walls and slabs
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires waterstops and shop drawings indicating their location; no explicit length given. Estimated based on typical below-grade wall and slab joint lengths for a commercial building.

takeoff_items.description: Shoring and bracing for formwork - lump sum allowance
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires shoring, bracing, and anchoring; no quantities given. Treated as lump sum pending structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Preinstallation conference - concrete forming (03 10 00)
takeoff_items.notes: source=specified

takeoff_items.description: Deformed steel reinforcing bars (rebar) - slabs, walls, footings, and beams
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit rebar tonnage or bar schedule given. Estimated at approximately 5 lb/SF for 15,000 SF of concrete structure, typical for commercial construction. Confirm from structural drawings and bar schedule.

takeoff_items.description: Welded wire reinforcement (WWR) - slabs on grade
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec includes WWR as a specified reinforcement type; no explicit area given. Estimated to match slab-on-grade area. Confirm from structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Steel fiber reinforcement (TSMR - Twisted Steel Micro Rebar) - concrete mix additive
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec specifies steel fiber / TSMR reinforcement with manufacturer dosage instructions; dosage rate and total concrete volume not stated. Treated as lump sum pending mix design and concrete volume confirmation.

takeoff_items.description: Mechanical splice couplers for reinforcing bars
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec references mechanical splice couplers as a product requiring submittals; no explicit count given. Estimated based on typical usage at construction joints for a commercial building.

takeoff_items.description: Bar supports (chairs, bolsters) for reinforcing steel
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires bar supports to maintain cover per ACI 318; no explicit count given. Quantity dependent on rebar layout from structural drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Preinstallation conference - concrete reinforcing (03 20 00)
takeoff_items.notes: source=specified

takeoff_items.description: Precast structural concrete units - erection and installation
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec covers installation of precast structural units; no unit count, panel count, or square footage given. Treated as lump sum pending precast shop drawings and erection plan.

takeoff_items.description: Bearing pads for precast structural concrete connections
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires bearing pads at connections; no count given. Estimated based on typical precast structural frame for a commercial building.

takeoff_items.description: Welding at precast structural concrete connections (AWS D1.1/D1.4)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires welding at connections per AWS D1.1 and D1.4; no weld length or connection count given. Treated as lump sum.

takeoff_items.description: Bolted connections for precast structural concrete (including lock washers)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec references bolted connections with lock washers; no count given. Estimated for typical precast structural frame.

takeoff_items.description: Non-shrink grout at precast structural concrete connections and joints
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires grouting of connections; no volume given. Estimated based on typical precast connection grouting for a commercial structure.

takeoff_items.description: Temporary shoring and bracing for precast structural concrete erection
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires temporary structural framing, shoring, and bracing during erection; no quantities given.

takeoff_items.description: Precast architectural concrete units - fabrication and installation
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec covers precast architectural concrete units; no panel count, area, or linear footage given. Treated as lump sum pending shop drawings.

takeoff_items.description: Precast architectural concrete sample panels (minimum 2 panels, approx. 16 SF each)

takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires bearing pads at connections; no count given. Estimated for typical architectural precast facade on a commercial building.

takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires grouting or dry packing of connections and joints; no volume given. Estimated for typical architectural precast facade.

takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec specifies uniform 3/4-inch joint width; total joint length not given. Estimated based on typical precast facade panel layout for a commercial building.

takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Spec requires temporary supports and bracing during erection; no quantities given.

takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | No explicit area given in spec. Estimated based on typical interior floor area of a commercial building requiring underlayment below finish flooring. Confirm from finish floor plan.

takeoff_items.description: Hydraulic cement underlayment average thickness (estimated 1/4 inch average depth)
takeoff_items.notes: source=estimated | Derived from estimated 12,000 SF at 1/4-inch average thickness: 12,000 x 0.0208 ft = 250 CF / 27 = 9.3 CY. Thickness and area to be confirmed from drawings and floor finish schedule.

estimate.executive_summary:
**Executive Summary — PRJ-2026-017 | Commercial Concrete Scope**

This bid proposal covers the complete concrete scope of work (CSI Division 03) for commercial project PRJ-2026-017, encompassing concrete forming, reinforcing steel placement, precast erection, and specialty concrete systems. Major scope items include approximately 7,500 SF of concrete forming and 90,000+ LB of rebar placement by carpenter and ironworker crews, precast erection operations under CSI 03 41 00 and 03 45 00, and a substantial volume of work under CSI 03 54 16 (underlayment/specialty concrete systems), which represents the single largest cost driver in the estimate at $3,432,000 for the primary line item alone. Total direct costs are carried at **$4,011,268**, with labor comprising the dominant share at **$3,505,423.60** and materials at **$155,302.05**. Applied markups include 10% overhead (**$401,126.80**), 8% profit (**$352,991.58**), and a 5% contingency (**$238,269.32**), resulting in a **Total Bid Amount of $5,003,655.70**. No bid bond is required for this submission.

Several assumptions and exclusions material to this proposal should be noted by the Owner and Construction Manager. All work is priced for normal business hours (7:00 AM–3:30 PM, Monday through Friday); overtime or shift premiums are excluded unless specifically directed and compensated. Permits, testing and inspection services, and site utilities are assumed to be Owner-provided. All quantities are based on plan takeoff from the referenced specification document, and field verification is recommended prior to contract execution. Material pricing carries a 30-day validity window from the estimate date. Excluded from this proposal are all MEP systems (Divisions 21–23 and 26), earthwork and site grading (Division 31), exterior improvements (Division 32), hazardous material abatement, and FF&E.

Our firm brings extensive experience in commercial concrete construction, with a proven track record of delivering complex forming, reinforcing, and specialty concrete scopes on schedule and within budget. We are confident that this proposal reflects accurate, competitive pricing consistent with current market conditions, and we welcome the opportunity to discuss the details of our approach with your project team.
